name: Expensify
when: Comes up when a group has already looked at a US-built expense tool
angle: US-centric, relies on existing bank card feeds rather than true card issuance, so there's a lag on data and no way to actually control spend upfront. A common near-miss for AU multi-site groups that evaluate it, then don't proceed.

name: SAP Concur
when: Evaluated by larger multi-site groups doing a formal software review
angle: Expensive and heavyweight, usually rejected on cost — especially when the prospect is currently on a "free" bank card portal. The real objection isn't features, it's justifying spend on top of something free.

name: Navan
when: Surfaces when a group is also shopping for travel management
angle: Can't issue debit cards in Australia and doesn't touch accounts payable. Structurally can't cover the same ground as Weel — a clean knockout when it comes up.

name: Bank-native card portals
when: Westpac Spend Clarity, CBA business card exports, and similar — almost every prospect has one
angle: Free or near-free, which is exactly why the prospect hasn't paid for anything yet. Clunky coding UX, no automated receipt chasing, no card-blocking. Usually the actual thing you're replacing, even when they say they "haven't looked at competitors."

name: ApprovalMax
when: Already in place at some multi-site dental and clinic groups for supplier invoice approval
angle: Position Weel for card and petty-cash-style spend plus AP automation, working alongside an existing ApprovalMax setup — not ripping it out where it's already working.
